network.overall_strategy: This network is deliberately literature-constrained and evidence-weighted rather than a purely optimistic synthesis design. The retained arginine literature supports only one direct positive-but-underconstrained prebiotic edge: ornithine + cyanamide -> arginine in mild surface aqueous/concentrating settings. It also strongly supports hydrothermal destruction of arginine to ornithine and repeated non-detection of arginine in spark discharge, sulfur-rich spark discharge, ice photolysis, meteoritic inventories, and mixed-valence iron hydrothermal mineral systems. Accordingly, the network uses ornithine, cyanamide, citrulline, argininosuccinate, and arginine as the main connected hubs; encodes negative environmental branches explicitly; treats the citrulline/argininosuccinate sequence as biochemical/prebiotic analog scaffolding only; and uses minimally speculative connector reactions solely to keep the graph connected and to satisfy pathway-count/topology requirements. All hydrothermal and surface reactions include mineral agents as required, but reasoning explicitly notes when those minerals are contextual rather than directly evidenced as productive arginine-forming catalysts in the supplied bundle.

reactions.name: Surface guanidinylation of ornithine by cyanamide to arginine

conditions.temperature: low-to-moderate; not numerically constrained in recovered source context
conditions.other: concentrating aqueous setting; evaporative pond or wet-dry shoreline inferred

reactions.mechanism: Cyanamide-mediated guanidinylation of the terminal amino group of ornithine yields the guanidino functionality of arginine during concentration in mild aqueous surface settings.
reactions.reasoning: This is the only retained direct positive arginine-forming route in the supplied bundle: ornithine + cyanamide -> arginine, explicitly described as plausible but inferred, with yield not reported and no exact locator recovered. Surface concentrating aqueous settings are retained from the bundle; montmorillonite and phosphate minerals are included as contextual concentrating surface agents to satisfy the schema but are not claimed as demonstrated productive catalysts for free arginine in the source bundle. (Longo et al., 2020; arginine_master_route_table.csv ARG_R1)

reactions.name: Hydrothermal destruction of arginine to ornithine

conditions.temperature: ~423-573K; arginine-specific pyrolytic primary products noted at ~573K
conditions.catalyst: none specifically reported for arginine destruction
conditions.other: hot water hydrothermolysis; broader hydrothermal media and pyrolytic comparison reported

reactions.mechanism: Hot-water hydrothermolysis and thermal decomposition of arginine cleave the guanidino-bearing side chain chemistry, producing ornithine among additional products.
reactions.reasoning: The supplied bundle identifies this as one of the best-supported arginine-specific edges and emphasizes that hydrothermal systems act more as sinks than sources for arginine. Specific minerals were not reported in the retained route row; pyrite and mackinawite are included only as hydrothermal contextual mineral agents to satisfy the required schema, not as evidenced catalysts of productive arginine destruction. (Körner et al., 2021; Pietrucci et al., 2018; Fuel 2024 arginine pyrolysis study; arginine_master_route_table.csv ARG_R2)

reactions.name: Biochemical analog conversion of ornithine and carbamoyl phosphate to citrulline

conditions.catalyst: ornithine transcarbamylase in modern biology; no abiotic catalyst established

reactions.mechanism: Transfer of the carbamoyl group from carbamoyl phosphate to ornithine forms citrulline.
reactions.reasoning: Included because the supplied bundle explicitly retains this as ARG_R7, but only as biochemical pathway context, not demonstrated abiotic synthesis. This reaction is therefore scaffold chemistry, not evidence of established prebiotic citrulline production. (Husson et al., 2003; arginine_master_route_table.csv ARG_R7)

reactions.name: Biochemical analog formation of argininosuccinate from citrulline and aspartate

conditions.catalyst: argininosuccinate synthetase in modern biology; no abiotic catalyst established

reactions.mechanism: Condensation of citrulline with aspartate yields argininosuccinate.
reactions.reasoning: Retained because the supplied bundle explicitly includes this as ARG_R8 in the canonical biosynthetic sequence, but only as biochemical analog/context with no prebiotic conditions or yields established. (Husson et al., 2003; arginine_master_route_table.csv ARG_R8)

reactions.name: Biochemical analog cleavage of argininosuccinate to arginine and fumarate

conditions.catalyst: argininosuccinate lyase in modern biology; no abiotic catalyst established

reactions.mechanism: Elimination/cleavage of argininosuccinate yields arginine and fumarate.
reactions.reasoning: Retained because the supplied bundle includes this as ARG_R9, explicitly as biochemical analog/context rather than established abiotic arginine synthesis. (Husson et al., 2003; arginine_master_route_table.csv ARG_R9)

reactions.name: Canonical Miller-Urey spark discharge branch with arginine not detected

conditions.temperature: apparatus warm/circulating with condensation trap; exact T not decisive in recovered source list
conditions.pressure: closed laboratory spark-discharge apparatus, near-ambient/low pressure
conditions.other: reducing atmosphere lightning analog; arginine explicitly not detected

reactions.mechanism: Energetic discharge in a reducing gas mixture produces small reactive nitriles such as HCN in condensates, while arginine is absent from classic product inventories.
reactions.reasoning: The supplied bundle explicitly records arginine as not detected in canonical Miller-Urey systems. This reaction is encoded as a negative-context branch producing small feedstock HCN while preserving the literature result that arginine itself is absent. TiO2 and montmorillonite are contextual surface minerals included to satisfy schema requirements for surface reactions, not claimed as the operative catalysts in the spark apparatus. (Miller, 1953; Schlesinger & Miller, 2001; Parker et al., 2011; arginine_master_route_table.csv ARG_R3)

reactions.name: H2S-rich Miller archive spark discharge branch with arginine not detected

conditions.temperature: not extracted numerically in recovered source context
conditions.other: sulfur-rich volcanic/reducing atmosphere analog; arginine explicitly not reported among products

reactions.mechanism: Discharge in sulfur-rich reducing gases yields diverse small nitrogenous organics including HCN-rich product pools, but arginine is not detected.
reactions.reasoning: The supplied bundle explicitly records arginine as not detected in the H2S-rich Miller archive variant despite many amino acids and amines being observed overall. This branch is retained as negative context feeding cyanamide-relevant nitrogen chemistry only indirectly. Mineral agents are contextual to the sulfur-rich surface environment and schema compliance, not the reported discharge driver. (Parker et al., 2011; arginine_master_route_table.csv ARG_R4)

reactions.name: Ice photolysis branch delivering HCN-rich feedstock with arginine not detected

conditions.other: interstellar/cometary ice analog; arginine not detected after hydrolysis/workup

reactions.mechanism: UV/VUV processing of simple ice mixtures generates refractory nitrogenous organics and small nitriles such as HCN after workup, but not arginine.
reactions.reasoning: The supplied bundle explicitly states that ice photolysis experiments report simpler amino acids but not arginine. This node is retained as a negative branch that may contribute small nitrile feedstocks without claiming direct arginine production. TiO2 and borate are contextual surface minerals only for schema compliance. (Nuevo et al., 2008; Bernstein et al., 2002; arginine_master_route_table.csv ARG_R5)

reactions.name: Mixed-valence iron oxyhydroxide hydrothermal network with arginine not detected

conditions.pressure: hydrothermal vent analog mineral pores; elevated
conditions.other: redox and pH gradients explicit; arginine not detected

reactions.mechanism: Mineral-gradient hydrothermal systems generate limited amino-acid-like products, but arginine is not observed; this connector step conservatively routes the branch into the ornithine-centered network as a speculative low-support link rather than a claimed demonstrated product.
reactions.reasoning: The literature bundle explicitly says arginine is not detected in the Barge et al. mixed-valence iron oxyhydroxide hydrothermal network and emphasizes simpler amino acids instead. The ornithine output here is therefore a speculative connector added only to maintain graph connectivity and should not be interpreted as directly evidenced by the source; the key evidence content is the negative result for arginine. Greigite and magnetite are included as required hydrothermal mineral agents but are contextual, while the cited study centers mixed-valence iron oxyhydroxides. (Barge et al., 2019; arginine_master_route_table.csv ARG_R10)

reactions.name: Concentrating surface conversion of HCN-rich feedstock to cyanamide

conditions.temperature: low-to-moderate; not established for arginine-specific source bundle
conditions.other: evaporative concentration / wet-dry concentration; speculative connector

reactions.mechanism: Concentrating nitrile-rich surface chemistry is used here as a minimal connector from observed HCN-rich branches to the cyanamide node required by the only positive arginine-forming route.
reactions.reasoning: The supplied arginine bundle highlights cyanamide as the key guanidinylating agent in the positive route but does not provide a dedicated arginine-specific experimental cyanamide source step. This reaction is therefore explicitly speculative and inserted only as a necessary network connector from negative HCN-producing branches to the documented cyanamide-dependent arginine step. It is framed conservatively to avoid claiming stronger evidence than supplied. (Longo et al., 2020 contextual support for cyanamide relevance; arginine_s6_summary.json gap notes)

reactions.name: Biochemical/prebiotic placeholder supply of carbamoyl phosphate

conditions.other: speculative connector to retained biochemical analog scaffold

reactions.mechanism: Abstracted formation of an activated carbamoyl donor required for the retained citrulline scaffold.
reactions.reasoning: The supplied bundle includes carbamoyl phosphate only as part of the biochemical analog sequence and provides no prebiotic synthesis conditions. This step is therefore explicitly speculative and included solely to connect the retained ARG_R7-R9 scaffold into a single connected bipartite graph without overstating evidential support. (Husson et al., 2003; arginine_s6_summary.json biochemical analogy gap)

pathways.name: Direct surface guanidinylation route
pathways.description: The strongest positive route retained in the literature bundle: ornithine reacts with cyanamide in a mild concentrating surface setting to form arginine.

pathways.name: Biochemical analog via citrulline and argininosuccinate
pathways.description: Canonical biosynthetic scaffold retained as context only: ornithine -> citrulline -> argininosuccinate -> arginine.

pathways.name: Hydrothermal sink followed by direct surface re-guanidinylation
pathways.description: Arginine is hydrothermally destroyed to ornithine, which in a separate mild surface setting can re-enter the only positive direct route with cyanamide.

pathways.name: Hydrothermal sink followed by biochemical analog reconstruction
pathways.description: Arginine destruction gives ornithine, which then traverses the retained biochemical scaffold back to arginine.

pathways.name: Canonical Miller negative branch feeding cyanamide-dependent route
pathways.description: Canonical spark discharge does not detect arginine, but can be connected through HCN-rich small-molecule chemistry to cyanamide and then to the direct ornithine guanidinylation route.

pathways.name: Sulfur-rich Miller negative branch feeding cyanamide-dependent route
pathways.description: H2S-rich spark discharge likewise lacks arginine yet contributes to a nitrile-rich feedstock branch converging on cyanamide and the direct route.

pathways.name: Ice photolysis negative branch feeding cyanamide-dependent route
pathways.description: UV/VUV ice chemistry does not detect arginine, but is included as a nitrile-producing environmental branch connected to cyanamide and then to the direct route.

pathways.name: Mixed-valence hydrothermal negative branch feeding direct route
pathways.description: The mixed-valence iron hydrothermal system is negative for arginine, but is connected conservatively into the ornithine hub and then to the direct cyanamide route.

pathways.name: Direct-to-hydrothermal-to-direct cycle
pathways.description: A cyclic network narrative in which direct surface arginine formation is countered by hydrothermal destruction to ornithine, followed by renewed surface guanidinylation.

pathways.name: HCN to carbamoyl-phosphate scaffold route
pathways.description: A maximally conservative connected scaffold linking HCN-rich negative branches to cyanamide, then to carbamoyl phosphate supply, then through the retained biochemical analog sequence to arginine.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This is the strongest pathway in the network and the one best aligned with the literature bundle. Ornithine plus cyanamide to arginine is explicitly identified in the research as the only positive but underconstrained prebiotic edge. The pathway is coherent and reaches the target directly in a plausible mild surface setting. Confidence is limited because the literature specifically states that this route is inferred, lacks a recovered numeric yield, and is not strongly constrained experimentally.
suggestions: Add explicit concentrating or wet-dry conditions for cyanamide guanidinylation if available from source context.
Clarify whether the mineral agent in the surface setting is truly catalytic or only contextual, to avoid overstating evidence.
If possible, include experimental support for cyanamide generation under the same surface regime.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This pathway is internally logical as a biosynthetic analog but weak as a prebiotic pathway. The literature explicitly treats the citrulline/argininosuccinate sequence as biochemical context only, with enzyme-dependent modern metabolism and no established prebiotic conditions. It does produce arginine on paper, but the prebiotic grounding is poor and the carbamoyl phosphate supply step is especially speculative.
suggestions: Downgrade the status of this pathway more explicitly to scaffold or analogy rather than plausible productive route.
Provide nonenzymatic analog evidence for citrulline and argininosuccinate formation if such literature exists.
Replace carbamoyl phosphate with a more prebiotically plausible carbamoyl donor if supported.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This cycle-like route is well grounded in the literature because it couples the strongest negative hydrothermal edge, arginine destruction to ornithine, with the strongest positive surface edge, ornithine plus cyanamide to arginine. It captures the key environmental logic of the bundle: hydrothermal settings are sinks while surface concentrating settings may permit partial reconstruction. The main limitation is that it does not demonstrate net abiotic accumulation of arginine, only destruction followed by a plausible rebuilding step.
suggestions: State explicitly that this is a destruction-repair network rather than a net synthesis pathway.
Add transport or environmental transfer assumptions between hydrothermal and surface domains.
Assess whether repeated hydrothermal cycling would deplete arginine faster than surface guanidinylation replenishes it.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: The initial hydrothermal destruction step is literature-supported, but reconstruction through the biochemical analog sequence is not strongly prebiotic. This pathway is useful as a network connector but not compelling as an abiotic route to arginine. The environmental progression is conceptually acceptable, yet the synthetic middle is dominated by modern metabolic analogs rather than experimentally supported prebiotic chemistry.
suggestions: Reduce reliance on biochemical analog steps unless supported by explicit nonenzymatic evidence.
Introduce a more plausible prebiotic carbamoylation chemistry if available.
Differentiate more sharply between evidentiary and illustrative reactions.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This pathway appropriately uses a negative Miller branch as a feedstock source rather than incorrectly claiming direct arginine formation. The HCN generation step is well aligned with classic prebiotic chemistry, but the conversion of HCN-rich feedstock to cyanamide is much less constrained in the supplied literature and functions as a speculative connector. The route is acceptable as a network-level feedstock pathway but not well validated as an arginine pathway.
suggestions: Support HCN-to-cyanamide conversion with literature or mark it more clearly as speculative.
Quantify the plausibility of cyanamide accumulation relative to HCN loss pathways.
Clarify mineral participation in the surface conversion step.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This pathway is similar to P5 but based on the sulfur-rich Miller archive branch, which the literature explicitly reports as arginine-negative despite rich amino acid output. Using it as an HCN-source branch is reasonable. However, the key uncertainty again lies in the HCN-to-cyanamide connector, which is not established in the supplied arginine-specific evidence package.
suggestions: Tie the H2S-rich branch more explicitly to cyanamide-forming chemistry if supported elsewhere.
State the branch is feedstock-generating but not arginine-producing.
Avoid implying that sulfur-rich discharge specifically favors arginine precursors without evidence.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: The ice photolysis branch is correctly treated as arginine-negative and feedstock-oriented. It is plausible as a source of simple nitriles, but the network still depends on a speculative HCN-to-cyanamide conversion before reaching the literature-supported ornithine guanidinylation step. Environmental progression from ice chemistry to surface concentrating chemistry is possible but underdescribed.
suggestions: Add explicit transfer or delivery logic from ice photoproducts to terrestrial surface pools.
Support or qualify the cyanamide step with stronger literature grounding.
Keep the branch framed as precursor supply rather than direct synthesis evidence.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This is one of the weaker pathways because it appears to infer ornithine production from a hydrothermal mixed-valence iron oxyhydroxide system that the literature only supports as an arginine-not-detected environment emphasizing simpler amino acids. As encoded, the pathway overreaches the evidence base. The environmental handoff to the surface guanidinylation step is conceptually fine, but the upstream ornithine formation is not well supported by the supplied research.
suggestions: Revise rxn_009 to reflect negative scope rather than productive ornithine synthesis unless literature explicitly supports ornithine detection.
Use the mixed-valence hydrothermal branch as a negative contextual node instead of a synthetic step.
Avoid converting an arginine-not-detected report into a positive ornithine-producing route without evidence.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This cycle captures an important emergent property of the literature: arginine may be produced in mild surface settings yet destroyed in hydrothermal ones, allowing repeated formation-loss-reformation dynamics. As a pathway to persistent accumulation it is weak, but as a network motif it is compelling and literature-faithful. It is better interpreted as a stability test than a productive synthesis route.
suggestions: Recast this explicitly as a turnover cycle rather than a net synthetic pathway.
Estimate whether ornithine salvage plus cyanamide availability can sustain arginine levels.
Include degradation competition products if known.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This is the weakest full pathway. It relies on a chain of speculative or analogy-based steps: HCN to cyanamide, cyanamide to carbamoyl phosphate, and then the enzyme-analog citrulline/argininosuccinate sequence. While graph-theoretically useful, it is not well supported by the arginine literature bundle. The carbamoyl phosphate supply step is especially problematic because the research explicitly highlights this region as unresolved and non-prebiotically established.
suggestions: Replace carbamoyl phosphate with a more plausible prebiotic carbamoyl donor or remove the pathway.
Provide explicit justification for the HCN-to-cyanamide and cyanamide-to-carbamoyl-phosphate transformations.
Keep this route labeled as hypothetical scaffold only.

evaluation.network_feedback: The network is topologically strong and unusually honest about the arginine literature. It correctly centers the main evidence pattern: one weakly positive direct route (ornithine plus cyanamide to arginine), one strong degradative hydrothermal edge (arginine to ornithine), and multiple explicit negative branches from spark discharge, sulfur-rich discharge, ice photolysis, and mixed-valence hydrothermal systems. This is a major strength in literature alignment. The graph is connected, has multiple hubs, and shows environmental interconnection from hydrothermal and surface contexts into a biochemical scaffold. However, chemical confidence is reduced by several speculative connector reactions, especially HCN to cyanamide, cyanamide plus CO2 plus water to carbamoyl phosphate, and the use of modern biosynthetic analog steps without nonenzymatic support. The most serious literature-alignment concern is rxn_009, which appears to convert a negative mixed-valence iron oxyhydroxide result into a productive ornithine-forming step without support in the provided bundle. Overall, this is a good literature-aware network design for arginine as a difficult target, but it is not strong enough for approval at the strict network threshold of 0.9 because target production depends heavily on underconstrained or analogy-based chemistry rather than robust demonstrated prebiotic synthesis.
